files.role: Defines patterns excluded from AI context ingestion. Prevents sensitive data files (.env, *.csv, *.xlsx), dependency trees (node_modules/, .venv/), and build artefacts from polluting the analytical context window.

Master system blueprint. Documents the standardised tech stack, three-module pipeline architecture (Data Ingestion → VIF Calculation → Executive Reporting), data schema, VIF severity taxonomy, engineering principles, and step-by-step execution sequence.

Reproducible synthetic dataset generator. Produces marketing_budget_2025.csv with 200 rows of realistic marketing spend data. Intentionally seeds near-perfect collinearity (r > 0.98) between Social_Media_Spend and Influencer_Fees to stress-test the VIF detection engine. Fixed seed (42) guarantees identical output across all environments.

Canonical input dataset. Eight columns: TV_Spend, Radio_Spend, Social_Media_Spend, Influencer_Fees, Digital_Display_Spend, Email_Marketing_Spend, SEO_Budget, Monthly_Revenue. All values in USD. Contains the deliberate structural collinearity condition that drives the CRITICAL VIF alerts.

Entry point for Phase 1: Data Ingestion and Executive Summary Display. Loads and validates the dataset schema and dtypes, renders a full descriptive statistics table via tabulate (count, mean, std, min, P25, median, P75, max), and surfaces a Pearson correlation preview flagging multicollinearity candidates before any statistical computation begins.

Core analytical engine for Phase 2. Constructs the full-rank design matrix, appends an OLS intercept via statsmodels.tools.add_constant, and iterates over all predictor columns to compute VIF scores using statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence.variance_inflation_factor. Classifies each score against a four-tier severity taxonomy (ACCEPTABLE / ELEVATED / HIGH / CRITICAL) and renders a ranked results table to stdout. Also exports load_and_compute_vif() for consumption by report_generator.py.

Executive reporting layer for Phase 3. Orchestrates a five-page PDF document via fpdf2: (1) full-bleed navy cover page with severity key and epigraph, (2) Strategic Executive Summary articulating VIF > 10 as a capital efficiency risk, (3) colour-coded VIF results table with KPI summary boxes, (4) prioritised Recommended Action Plan with four technical remediation strategies, (5) Methodology and Technical Appendix with the VIF formula and threshold rationale. Loads Arial TTF from Windows system fonts to ensure full Unicode rendering.

Primary client-deliverable artefact. A five-page, professionally typeset executive PDF report summarising the VIF diagnostic findings, strategic implications, and prioritised remediation roadmap. Suitable for direct presentation to C-suite and CFO stakeholders. Generated by report_generator.py on each run; always reflects the latest VIF computation against the current dataset.

engineering_issues_resolved.root_cause: Windows default terminal encoding (cp1252) cannot render Unicode box-drawing characters produced by tabulate's 'rounded_outline' table format.
engineering_issues_resolved.resolution: Applied sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8') at startup in main.py and vif_calculator.py. Scripts also invoked with the Python -X utf8 flag for defence-in-depth.

engineering_issues_resolved.issue: fpdf2 FPDFUnicodeEncodingException on em dash and mathematical symbols
engineering_issues_resolved.root_cause: fpdf2's built-in core fonts (Helvetica, Times, Courier) are encoded in Latin-1 (ISO 8859-1), which covers only code points U+0000 through U+00FF. The em dash (U+2014), greater-than-or-equal sign U+2265, and other typographic characters used in the executive report text fall outside this range.
engineering_issues_resolved.resolution: Replaced all Helvetica references with Arial, loaded from the Windows system font directory (C:/Windows/Fonts/) via pdf.add_font() for Regular, Bold, Italic, and Bold-Italic styles. Arial is a full Unicode TTF font that supports the complete character set required by the report.

engineering_issues_resolved.root_cause: fpdf2 2.7.6 renamed the txt keyword argument to text in multi_cell() and related methods. Using the legacy name triggers deprecation warnings.
engineering_issues_resolved.resolution: Global find-and-replace of txt= to text= across all multi_cell() invocations in report_generator.py.

vif_audit_results.audit_verdict: CRITICAL — mandatory remediation required before any model deployment

next_phases.phase_2: Remediation Engine — implement composite index construction, PCA transformation, and post-remediation VIF recomputation to validate convergence below threshold.
next_phases.phase_3: Client Delivery Pipeline — parameterise report_generator.py to accept arbitrary datasets; add CLI argument parsing for white-label deployment across multiple client engagements.
